links.targetTitle: Bee Reactor Adapter requires full Bee node, mutable stamp, wallet, and open P2P ports
Postage stamp batches use 65,536 buckets distributed by the first 16 bits of chunk address

global.title: Adapter requires Bee v2.7.1+ with a depth-22+ stamp; bee dev lacks SOC/feeds so the adapter falls back to /bytes mode
global.content:
# Bee Node Prerequisites and Dev Mode Constraints

The adapter requires a running Bee node (v2.7.1+ recommended), a funded postage stamp of depth 22+ (~7.7 GB capacity), and an Ethereum wallet for key derivation.

Tests run against `bee dev` — a memory-only Bee node with no blockchain and no real tokens:

- Full HTTP API on port 1633
- Postage stamps work but are free
- Data is stored in memory only (lost on restart)
- **SOC/Feeds are not supported in dev mode** — the adapter automatically falls back to `/bytes` mode when it detects dev

This dev-mode `/bytes`-only fallback is a meaningful behavioral branch: the mutable-feed pointer pattern that powers production recovery does not actually exercise in dev tests; only the immutable upload path is covered.

A test stamp can be minted with:

```bash
curl -s -X POST http://localhost:1633/stamps/10000000/24 | jq .batchID
```

The 12 E2E tests live in the adapter package and require `bee dev` running in a separate terminal before `pnpm test`.
global.description: Adapter requires Bee v2.7.1+ with a depth-22+ stamp; bee dev lacks SOC/feeds so it falls back to /bytes mode only.
